source_refs.rationale: Taxat requires server-mediated browser sessions, separate native posture, and distinct machine credentials.
The IdP is a bootstrap boundary for authentication posture, not the engine of product legality.
Current Auth0 guidance recommends separate tenants for development, staging, and production environments.
Current Auth0 application settings guidance constrains callback, logout, origin, and native redirect posture.

tenant_records.residency_posture: EU residency posture selected to keep non-production and production identity material aligned with the earlier environment catalog.
tenant_records.automation_access_posture: Dashboard browser automation is permitted from the governed provisioning workspace only; runtime surfaces use app clients, not tenant-admin credentials.
notes: Taxat business-tenant context stays engine-owned and resolves after authentication, not inside the provider tenant layout.

typed_gaps: The shared operating contract shared_operating_contract_0038_to_0045.md was absent, so the topology grounded directly in ADR-003, the environment catalog, and current Auth0 documentation.
The vendor decision was not frozen by an earlier ADR, so the bootstrap used the Auth0-compatible default and recorded PROVIDER_DEFAULT_APPLIED explicitly.
notes: The selected topology uses three control-plane tenants: development for local/bootstrap surfaces, staging for sandbox plus pre-production runtime, and production for live runtime.
